title: Living in Boulder vlog 5 - 6 - 2017: Prepping lots of Greco Roman books for my site

enrichment.summary: Kyle vlogs about staying up until 6-7am adding Greco-Roman books to his website, sourcing text from WikiSource. He discusses his workflow for building out a book section, his plan to spend the coming week collecting more classical texts, and his interest in converting them to MP3 recordings. He also mentions planning to sell his car.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: deeply absorbed and motivated, treating it like a valued time investment
excited about the idea but notes copyright prevents direct conversion; recording via playback is the workaround
wants to build a comprehensive library, referencing the Library of Alexandria and Library of Congress as aspirations
prefers committing to week-long focused sprints rather than open-ended goals

transcript.text: Hello, living in Boulder vlog for May 6, 2017. I'm too lazy to do my green screen. I just got to do the video. Hopefully my roommate won't appear out of nowhere.

So, last night I was working all the way until like 8 in the morning or something like that, or 7 in the morning. It was like daytime. Then I went in my room and worked a little bit more and then I went to sleep. I'm doing a whole bunch of books. I decided to get all of the Greco-Roman books on my website because I can do it so much faster all of a sudden. I wish I had a faster computer before, but whatever.

And yeah, because when I get them all up, I think I can make it look like there's, like, it's not that much information and that, you know, I can wrap my head around it. You know, I'm like, I wish that there was more, you know.
And then you proceed to memorize all of it. And that would be fun.

When I have an entire book on my page, one cool thing that I can do with that is I can record it and I can play it and then record it, you know, like with my computer recording itself or my phone or something, mp3 recording it.

And, um, make mp3 files out of it. You can't just convert them because of, um, copyright laws, but, uh, to turn this stuff into mp3 would be really cool because then you could listen to them while you do, you know, mp3, while you're out writing and stuff.
You don't have to just do it at home, but, um, it's going to be awesome to be able to see exactly how big these books are and what they look like on my site.

So I did a whole bunch. Which ones did I do? Maybe I should read it out. Let's see here. I did, you know what, I won't read it out. It'll be boring. Um, but I did a whole bunch. I'm going to do everything. Um, after, right now I'm doing Strabo and then after this, I think I'm going to do Antiquities of the Jews and then, yeah, I'll probably just throw in.

I want to do all of Cicero because the speeches, those really let you make you bring you into the world. Um, so yeah, so I've just been listening to that. I'm having my computer read all the stuff from Wikisource that I'm converting onto my website. I'm only creating the book section, not the, um, not the, uh, chapters. Um, so, but yeah, I'll just paste those on.

So I was thinking about going through all of this week, just collecting books, like setting a time to it because, you know, cause like to me, it's like, how much is it worth to me? I think in terms currency is in terms of time. So I'm like, okay, give me another week. Then I do another week on this. Cause I only started, you know, all these different things like the end of last week. So you get a whole week. So yeah, man. And then that's not even, um, putting polishing them and completing them, you know?

Like the venues and stuff. That's just collecting the book section, text section of it. So, you know, maybe it'd take me like another week or something a few days after that to tie it all up. But I'd have, I like committing myself cause then I'd have a whole library, the Alexandrian library. Um, yeah, I want to make my little Alexandrian library. So, um,
Um, that, uh, yes, I think I'll go all the way through. It's Saturday now. I'll go all the way through next week from them to next Sunday. It's like a week from now.
Um, and then we'll turn that. We'll see what I can gather. And then we'll turn that into my website and then we'll get going on the timeline again. Uh, the Roman timeline.
Uh, I'm back in long haul. Somebody was interested in my car. I'm in the text and they're like, do a video. So yeah, I'm definitely going to do a video tomorrow.
Um, I wanted to today, but, um, I was too hooked on this cause it was only my second day collecting all these books.
And I was just like, Oh my God, look how many I'm getting. So I'll get them tomorrow.
I'll do the video on my car tomorrow and hopefully sell that thing. Uh, okay. Enough talking. Peace out.
